document.source_title: Global health gains face threat of reversal

claims.claim_text: The world is falling short on health targets.
claims.evidence_text: The world is falling short on health targets, with progress uneven, slowing, and in some areas reversing.

claims.claim_text: Progress towards universal health coverage has slowed sharply.
claims.evidence_text: Progress towards universal health coverage (UHC) has slowed sharply.

claims.claim_text: The COVID-19 pandemic linked to an estimated 22.1 million excess deaths.
claims.evidence_text: Between 2020 and 2023, it was linked to an estimated 22.1 million excess deaths, including indirect deaths.

metrics.name: HIV infections reduction
metrics.evidence_text: new HIV infections fell by 40% between 2010 and 2024

metrics.name: UHC service coverage index
metrics.evidence_text: the global UHC service coverage index rose only slightly from 68 to 71 between 2015 and 2023

metrics.name: Maternal mortality reduction
metrics.evidence_text: global maternal mortality has fallen by 40% since 2000

metrics.name: Anaemia prevalence
metrics.evidence_text: Anaemia affects 30.7% of women of reproductive age

metrics.name: Overweight prevalence among children
metrics.evidence_text: the prevalence of overweight among children under five reached 5.5% in 2024

metrics.name: Air pollution deaths
metrics.evidence_text: Air pollution contributed to an estimated 6.6 million deaths worldwide in 2021

metrics.name: Deaths from inadequate water, sanitation, and hygiene
metrics.evidence_text: inadequate water, sanitation and hygiene contributed to 1.4 million deaths in 2019

citations.title: Global health gains face threat of reversal

source_credibility.reason: Published by the World Health Organization
